name: Togo
experiences: Grand Marché and fetish-market energy in Lomé
avoid: April to October is wetter and slower, with muddy rural roads around Plateaux and the far north making bush-taxi timing less reliable.
Public transport across Togo is affordable but long; Lomé to Kara or Koutammakou easily consumes a full daylight leg even before last-mile moto-taxis.
Carry small cash notes for moto-taxis, shrine donations, market food, and Batammariba or park guide fees because card acceptance outside Lomé is limited.
stopoverNote: Lomé (LFW) is the natural gateway; most India trips work best as Lomé arrival and departure with one south-to-north overland sweep, because domestic links are limited and the best cultural stops sit on slow road corridors.

cities.name: Lomé
cities.notes: Seafront capital for markets, beach strips, fetish culture, and the easiest transport connections into the rest of Togo.

cities.name: Kpalimé
cities.notes: Cooler highland town for Mount Agou, waterfalls, cocoa-country day trips, and a gentler pace than Lomé.

cities.name: Koutammakou (Tamberma Valley)
cities.notes: UNESCO-listed Batammariba country of earthen tata somba houses, reached via Kara or Kandé with a slow but rewarding rural last mile.

cities.name: Togoville
cities.notes: Lake Togo spiritual town known for pirogue arrivals, vodun shrines, and a quiet lakeside rhythm east of Lomé.

itinerary.sim: Togocom is the safest all-route pick for Lomé, Kpalimé, Kara and Lake Togo; budget roughly ₹420–₹980 for a prepaid SIM or data bundle big enough for maps, WhatsApp and ride-hailing in the capital.

days.theme: Start with Lomé’s raw market energy, fetish lore, and easy city rides before leaving the coast.

activities.name: Gozem, city taxi, or airport taxi from Gnassingbé Eyadéma Airport into central Lomé
Grand Marché de Lomé walk
Marché des Féticheurs d’Akodésséwa guide and photo contribution
Cathédrale du Sacré-Cœur and Monument de l’Indépendance walk
Moto-taxi and city taxi hops across downtown and the beachfront

days.theme: Use a second day for cultural spaces, artisan shopping, and a slower Atlantic-facing finish.

activities.name: Palais de Lomé cultural center and gardens entry
Village Artisanal de Lomé shopping browse
Lomé beachfront and Hotel Sarakawa side walk
Gozem car or moto rides between the coast, Kodjoviakopé, and your hotel
Sunset drinks stop around Marcelo Beach or the boulevard

days.theme: Trade the coast for Togo’s green highlands and use Kpalimé for the country’s best-known summit hike.

activities.name: Bush taxi or intercity minibus from Lomé to Kpalimé
Mount Agou hike access fee
Local guide for the Mount Agou trail
Moto-taxi from Kpalimé to the Agou trailhead villages
Kpalimé artisan market or craft-centre browse after the hike

days.theme: Keep an extra day for waterfalls, cocoa-country visits, and a slower highland reset before the long northern transfer.

activities.name: Cascade de Kpimé entry and local guide
Coffee or cocoa farm visit outside Kpalimé
Moto-taxi loops between Kpalimé, Kpimé, and nearby viewpoints
Kpalimé market fruit, coffee, and craft shopping
Short forest walk with a local guide

days.theme: Make the long northbound push, using Kara as the staging base and folding in Togo’s biggest wilderness reserve if timing works.

activities.name: Intercity bus or bush taxi from Kpalimé toward Kara via Atakpamé and Sokodé
Fazao-Malfakassa National Park entry fee
Ranger or local guide for a Fazao-Malfakassa forest drive or walk
Bush taxi from Kara to Kandé or the Nadoba junction
Evening village-edge walk or viewpoint stop near your lodge

days.theme: Give the UNESCO landscape a full day for Batammariba architecture, guides, and slow travel between earthen towers.

activities.name: Koutammakou Cultural Landscape / tata somba house visit
Batammariba village guide and photo contribution
Moto-taxi between Nadoba hamlets and trailheads
Traditional granary, craft, or food demonstration donation
Sunset walk over the Tamberma Valley

days.theme: Finish by Lake Togo with pirogues, shrine visits, and a calmer spiritual atmosphere than the capital.

activities.name: Long-haul intercity bus or shared taxi south from Kara, then bush taxi onward to Agbodrafo
Pirogue across Lake Togo to Togoville
Togoville vodun shrine circuit with a local guide
Notre-Dame du Lac church and lakeside donation stop
Moto-taxi or bush taxi between Agbodrafo landing points and your hotel

connections.method: Bush taxis and intercity minibuses are the standard move inland, with moto-taxis useful for the final hill-village legs around Agou.
This is a full northbound transport day: intercity bus or shared taxi toward Kara, then a bush taxi or moto-taxi for the last rural stretch toward Nadoba.
Return south by bus or shared taxi through Kara and Lomé, then continue east to Agbodrafo before the short pirogue crossing to Togoville.
Pirogue back to the lakeshore followed by a bush taxi or shared car west to Lomé.

extras: November to March is the cleanest all-round window: trails on Mount Agou are drier, northbound roads are easier, and Lake Togo crossings feel simpler than in the wet season.
Moto-taxis are everywhere in Lomé, Kpalimé, Kara and the lakeside towns; agree the fare before you climb on and carry a scarf or dust layer for long rides.
Koutammakou visits work best with a local guide because access, photo etiquette, and village contributions are community-managed rather than ticket-desk formalities.
Fazao-Malfakassa is remote and is best treated as a ranger-led detour while already moving north, not as a casual day trip from the coast.
Grand Marché, shrine visits, pirogues, and bush taxis are all strongly cash-first, so keep small CFA notes available outside your main hotel payments.
